result.summary: archive.org exposes public HTTP APIs for Wayback, search, metadata, views, changes, tasks, reviews, and an S3-like upload API, plus the `ia` CLI; public read surfaces are unauthenticated while write/user/task surfaces use IA-S3 credentials.
result.description: Internet Archive is a digital library and web archiving service that stores books, media, software, and archived web pages. It provides developer access to search, metadata, upload, review, task, and Wayback-related services for working with archive.org content.

ia_s3_keys.label: IA-S3 access key and secret key
ia_s3_keys.setup: Sign in to your [Internet Archive account](https://archive.org/account/login), then open [IA-S3 Keys](https://archive.org/account/s3.php). Check the acknowledgment box and click **Generate New Keys** to get an access key and secret key pair. The developer tutorial says these keys are used for uploading, searching, and modifying metadata.

surfaces.name: Internet Archive Views Data Service API
